decisions.decision: min_asset_check() raises ApiError(422, 'min_asset_violation', ...) instead of using build_terminal_rejection_response(409). This changes the rejection semantics from terminal command rejection (persisted CommandRejected event with idempotency replay) to transient validation error (no event, no replay, retries re-evaluate).
alternatives_rejected: Keep build_terminal_rejection_response but change status to 422 — rejected because the task explicitly says 'ApiError status 422', and min_asset violations are pre-flight solvency checks, not terminal business events
Return response tuple from helper — rejected because raise-based ApiError pattern is used 50+ times in server.py and the task specifies ApiError semantics

decisions.decision: Helper signature is min_asset_check(asset_preview: dict[str, Any]) -> None. Receives pre-computed asset_preview dict from preview_account_min_asset. The caller decides WHEN to check; the helper decides IF the check passes.
alternatives_rejected: Pass individual fields (account_id, market_id, command_id, etc.) — rejected because asset_preview already bundles all needed fields and decouples from specific edit types
Have helper call preview_account_min_asset internally — rejected because it would couple the helper to one preview type

decisions.decision: Keep the min_asset check in its current code position (after command creation, after market_not_active check). Even though ApiError semantics are pre-flight, moving the check before command creation would also move it before the market_not_active check, causing wasted preview computation on inactive markets.
alternatives_rejected: Move check before command creation — rejected because it would run the preview and min_asset check even for inactive markets (market_not_active check is currently between command creation and min_asset check, and it depends on the command for build_terminal_rejection_response)

decisions.decision: ApiError details dict includes only asset_preview fields (accountId, riskLimit, beforeMinAsset, impactScore, afterMinAsset). Drops marketId and commandId that were in the old terminal rejection details, since these are command-lifecycle concerns and the ApiError is a validation error.
alternatives_rejected: Add market_id/command_id params to helper — rejected because ApiError semantics don't need command context, and marketId is available from the URL path

decisions.decision: Preserve the strict less-than check (afterMinAsset < 0). The boundary condition afterMinAsset == 0 is accepted, matching current behavior. This is a regression guard per q3.

steps.task: Define min_asset_check(asset_preview: dict) -> None helper in server.py near preview_account_min_asset (around line 1713). The function checks asset_preview['afterMinAsset'] < 0 and raises ApiError(422, 'min_asset_violation', 'Edit would produce negative state-contingent assets', details=asset_preview).

Replace inline min_asset guard at server.py:4158-4175 with a single call: min_asset_check(asset_preview). Removes 17 lines (the if-block with build_terminal_rejection_response), adds 1 line (the function call).

Update unit tests for new rejection contract. Tests affected: (a) test_probability_edit_rejects_unconditional_min_asset_violation_without_side_effects (line 2691) — change status 409->422, remove result/event assertions (no CommandRejected event, no result block), update details to match asset_preview-only fields, update COMMANDS/EVENTS count assertions (no command or event created for ApiError path). (b) test_probability_edit_replays_unconditional_min_asset_rejection (line 2766) — rewrite to verify retry re-evaluates the check (no idempotency replay; second request gets a fresh 422 ApiError, not a replayed response). (c) test_probability_edit_replay_returns_stored_min_asset_rejection_contract (line 2800) — rewrite to verify retry re-evaluates (no terminal outcome stored, no replay).

Update property tests for new contract. Tests affected: (a) test_unconditional_probability_edit_property_rejects_negative_headroom (line 5410) — change 409->422, remove result block assertions, update details assertions. (b) test_unconditional_probability_edit_property_second_edit_exhausts_headroom (line ~5560) — change 409->422, remove result/reasonCode assertions. (c) test_unconditional_probability_edit_property_preserves_valid_marginal_distribution (line 5632) — change 409->422, remove result block assertions.

Update HTTP integration tests for new contract. Tests affected: (a) test_probability_edit_http_rejects_unconditional_min_asset_violation (line 8423) — change 409->422, remove result/event/replay assertions (ApiError is transient, no idempotency replay). (b) test_probability_edit_http_replay_preserves_min_asset_rejection_contract (line 8462) — rewrite to verify re-evaluation instead of replay. (c) test_probability_edit_http_journals_unconditional_rejection_in_event_stream (line ~8530) — change 409->422, remove CommandRejected event-stream assertions (no event is appended). (d) test_probability_edit_http_accepts_zero_min_asset_boundary (line 8641) — verify unchanged (boundary acceptance, no regression).

Update invariant tests for new contract. Tests affected: (a) test_invariant_rejection_appends_rejection_event_and_preserves_state (line ~5940) — change 409->422, remove CommandRejected event assertions, remove event-stream assertions (no event is created). (b) test_invariant_first_market_event_can_be_rejection_without_state_mutation (line 6038) — change 409->422, remove event assertions.
Run full test suite (python -m pytest tests/) and fix any missed assertion failures or regressions.

decomposition_assessment.decision_reason: Estimated total lines (281) is under 500. Zero high-risk steps. While steps 3-6 (test updates) are logically independent, they all modify the same test file (test_bayes_market_api.py) so parallel agents would create merge conflicts. The changes are mechanical (status code, response shape, remove event assertions) and straightforward for a single worker.
